title: Built the AI Spend (vendor-spend) module end-to-end
summary: Executed the vendor-spend plan (artifacts/platform/2026-06-30-vendor-spend-plan/SPEC.md): committed ten-vendor registry, migration 014 (budgets / cost snapshots with idempotent api-daily upserts / refresh audit log), src/lib/vendor-spend domain (UTC period math with monthly budget-cap proration, warn 80% / critical 95% / over alert evaluator, Postgres store with memory fallback), AWS Cost Explorer + Anthropic Admin cost_report + Cursor Admin /teams/spend adapters that degrade visibly instead of throwing or fabricating spend, /api/vendor-spend routes plus CRON_SECRET-gated cron refresh, the full AI Spend screen (scariest-first table, period filter, budget editor, manual entry, degraded panels, sidebar alert badge), integrations.yaml declaration, and the v2 alerts follow-up bundle. ./scripts/preflight.sh --mode pre-push exits 0 (786 unit tests, 162 e2e).
decisions: Migration landed as 014_vendor_spend.sql — the drafted 011 prefix was already taken by 011_projects.sql
Store falls back to process-local memory without PLX_MC_DATABASE_URL (same contract as the skills submissions store) so tests and the e2e dev server need no database
Cursor adapter records ONE billing-cycle observation keyed on the cycle start (the Admin API only reports the current cycle), always flagged estimated / usage-reported
Monthly budgets are caps: calendar-anchored periods count each touched month as a full month of budget; only weekly windows prorate by days
Client components import only types from the vendor-spend barrel — the barrel is server-only because the loader pulls node:fs
lessons: Next 16 build fails when a client component imports a runtime value from a barrel that transitively reaches node:fs — keep display-order constants in the UI helpers
The repo's react-hooks/set-state-in-effect rule forbids sync setState in effect bodies; fetch-on-mount must set state only in promise continuations
vi.restoreAllMocks does not clear vi.fn call history — use vi.clearAllMocks in beforeEach when asserting not.toHaveBeenCalled
